title: micropholis submarginalis
intro: Micropholis submarginalis, also known as the White Titi tree, is a species of plant that belongs to the Sapotaceae family and is native to the tropical forests of Central and South America. The bark and leaves of the plant have been traditionally used for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits associated with Micropholis submarginalis include reducing fever, treating respiratory infections, and supporting the immune system. The plant is typically prepared as a decoction or tea by boiling the bark and leaves in water, or as a tincture by mixing the plant material with a solvent like ethanol or glycerin.
